title: American Sloppy Joe-Style Keema Pav

subs.name: finely mashed rajma (kidney beans) or soya granules (soaked) for a vegan version; same technique, same cook time
local pav (the Indian dinner roll) is ideal — use two per serving for the authentic Indian-American fusion experience
equal parts tomato paste and a teaspoon of jaggery for a less sweet, more robust sauce
a squeeze of lemon (nimbu) — adds the same tartness at the end

steps.text: Heat oil in a kadai on medium flame. Add the cumin seeds and let them sizzle for 15 seconds. Add the diced onion and cook, stirring, for 4–5 minutes until soft and lightly coloured.
Add the garlic and fry for 1 minute until fragrant. Add the capsicum and stir for 2 minutes until slightly softened.

Add the keema and break it up immediately with a spoon so there are no large clumps. Cook on medium-high, stirring and pressing, for 5–6 minutes until the raw colour disappears completely and the meat starts to brown.
Add the chopped tomatoes, red chilli powder and salt. Stir well, lower the flame to medium and cook for 5 minutes until the tomatoes collapse and the oil begins to separate at the edges.

Stir in the ketchup and vinegar. Add a quarter cup of water and simmer for 5 minutes, stirring, until the sauce is thick, glossy and clings to the meat rather than pooling at the bottom. Taste and adjust salt and vinegar.

Toast the pav halves in the same kadai with a smear of oil or butter until golden on the cut side. Spoon the keema filling generously onto the bottom halves — it should overflow slightly. Scatter coriander leaves over the top and cap with the upper half. Serve with extra ketchup.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Keep raw animal products, their plates and utensils separate from cooked food; wash hands and surfaces after contact.
critical_checks: Use an appropriate food thermometer and safe doneness guidance for the specific cut of meat.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: keema → finely mashed rajma (kidney beans) or soya granules (soaked) for a vegan version; same technique, same cook time; bread → local pav (the Indian dinner roll) is ideal — use two per serving for the authentic Indian-American fusion experience; tomato-ketchup → equal parts tomato paste and a teaspoon of jaggery for a less sweet, more robust sauce; vinegar → a squeeze of lemon (nimbu) — adds the same tartness at the end
indian_kitchen_adaptation: This recipe is designed for a kadai from the first step — the wide base helps the keema brown rather than steam. Local pav (available at any bakery) is superior to burger buns for absorbing the saucy filling. Kissan or Maggi ketchup is perfect. Minced chicken (murgh keema) is available at most meat shops and cooks faster than mutton; ask the butcher to mince it fine.
